description: NFT commands — mint, burn, modify, and trade NFTs on the XRP Ledger with full offer lifecycle support

userStories.title: nft mint command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to mint an NFT from the CLI so I can create on-chain digital assets with configurable properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Read NFTokenMint docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/nftokenmint.md before implementing
Create src/commands/nft.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts

xrpl nft mint subcommand with: --taxon n (required, UInt32), --uri string (optional, plain string converted to hex via convertStringToHex from xrpl), --transfer-fee bps (optional, 0-50000; requires --transferable — CLI errors if used without it), --burnable flag (tfBurnable), --only-xrp flag (tfOnlyXRP), --transferable flag (tfTransferable), --mutable flag (tfMutable), --issuer address (optional)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if --transfer-fee is set without --transferable
Do NOT use tfTrustLine flag (0x00000004) — deprecated and invalid
On success, output prints the NFTokenID from tx.result.meta.nftoken_id (or scan AffectedNodes for CreatedNode with LedgerEntryType NFTokenPage as fallback)
tests/e2e/nft/nft.validation.test.ts: missing --taxon, --transfer-fee > 50000, --transfer-fee without --transferable, missing key material (no network)
tests/e2e/nft/nft.mint.test.ts: mint with taxon only, mint with --uri, mint with --transfer-fee + --transferable, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: nft burn and nft modify commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to burn an NFT and update a mutable NFT's URI to manage token lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Read NFTokenBurn docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/nftokenburn.md before implementing
Read NFTokenModify docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/nftokenmodify.md before implementing
xrpl nft burn subcommand with: --nft hex (required, 64-char NFTokenID), --owner address (optional, for issuer burning a burnable token they don't hold)
xrpl nft modify subcommand with: --nft hex (required), --uri string (optional, plain string hex-encoded), --clear-uri flag (explicitly deletes existing URI), --owner address (optional)
CLI exits with error if neither --uri nor --clear-uri is provided
CLI exits with error if both --uri and --clear-uri are provided
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
tests/e2e/nft/nft.burn.test.ts: mint then burn an NFT, --json, --dry-run
tests/e2e/nft/nft.modify.test.ts: mint with --mutable, modify URI, verify via account nfts, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: nft offer create command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to create buy or sell offers for NFTs from the CLI to participate in the marketplace.
acceptanceCriteria: Read NFTokenCreateOffer docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/nftokencreateoffer.md before implementing

xrpl nft offer create subcommand with: --nft hex (required), --amount amount (required, XRP decimal or value/CURRENCY/issuer; '0' valid for XRP sell giveaways), --sell flag (sets tfSellNFToken; absence = buy offer), --owner address (required for buy offers — CLI validates), --expiration ISO8601 (optional, converted to XRPL epoch), --destination address (optional)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI exits with error if creating a buy offer (no --sell) without --owner
On success, output prints the NFTokenOfferID from AffectedNodes CreatedNode with LedgerEntryType NFTokenOffer
Reuse parseAmount and toXrplAmount from src/utils/amount.ts
tests/e2e/nft/nft.offer.create.test.ts: create sell offer, create buy offer, --expiration, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: nft offer accept and nft offer cancel commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to accept NFT offers in direct or brokered mode and cancel outstanding offers.
acceptanceCriteria: Read NFTokenAcceptOffer docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/nftokenacceptoffer.md before implementing
Read NFTokenCancelOffer docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/nftokencanceloffer.md before implementing
xrpl nft offer accept subcommand with: --sell-offer hex (optional), --buy-offer hex (optional), --broker-fee amount (optional, XRP or IOU, only valid with both offer IDs)
CLI exits with error if neither --sell-offer nor --buy-offer is provided
CLI exits with error if --broker-fee is provided without both --sell-offer and --buy-offer
xrpl nft offer cancel subcommand with: --offer hex (repeatable), e.g. --offer <id1> --offer <id2>
CLI exits with error if no --offer is provided
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
tests/e2e/nft/nft.offer.accept.test.ts: accept a sell offer (direct), accept a buy offer (direct), brokered mode accept, --json, --dry-run
tests/e2e/nft/nft.offer.cancel.test.ts: cancel a single offer, cancel multiple offers in one tx, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: nft offer list query command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to list all buy and sell offers for a specific NFT so I can decide which to accept or cancel.
acceptanceCriteria: xrpl nft offer list <nft-id> subcommand fetching both nft_sell_offers and nft_buy_offers RPCs in parallel

Default human-readable output has two sections 'Sell Offers' and 'Buy Offers'; each offer shows: offer ID, amount (XRP decimal or IOU), owner, expiration (ISO8601 or 'none'), destination (address or 'any')
--json outputs { sellOffers: [...], buyOffers: [...] }
--node supported
tests/e2e/nft/nft.offer.list.test.ts: mint NFT, create sell and buy offers, verify both appear in list; --json output
Typecheck passes
